text: Verify-and-rollback gate for self-redeploy: a bad self-edit must not brick the runtime
description:
The safety property that makes recursive self-improvement viable. When the agent edits its own supervisor/watchdog code and triggers a self-rebuild+relaunch (parent aim 5af61b3e), two guards must protect the runtime:

1. PRE-RELAUNCH VERIFY GATE: before relaunching, run typecheck + the wrapped-agents tests (e.g. `npm run type-check` and `npm test -w @aimparency/wrapped-agents-common`). If either fails, do NOT relaunch — keep running the current (old) code and tell the agent its change is unsafe. Build/typecheck success alone is insufficient; tests must pass.

2. POST-RELAUNCH ROLLBACK (don't brick yourself): today session-server's relaunchOnNonZeroExit just relaunches the SAME code, which crash-loops on a bad self-edit. Detect a boot-failure loop (the relaunched session fails to reach a healthy heartbeat within N seconds, or exits non-zero M times) and automatically `git`-revert the offending commit to the last-known-good, rebuild, relaunch, and escalate to the human (set a human-dependent flag with the failing diff). The agent must be recoverable from one bad commit, not killed by it.

3. AUDIT: record each self-redeploy attempt — commit hash, verify result, boot outcome (healthy / rolled-back) — into session memory / agent_log for auditability.

Grounded in this session: four watchdog self-fixes each needed a manual human restart to deploy (see memory: dev sessions run via `--import tsx`, no watch). Primitive already exists: broker.relaunch (manager.ts:418) + relaunchOnNonZeroExit. May split into 'pre-relaunch verify gate' and 'boot-failure rollback watchdog'.
